revision_reason: ADJ-013 needs_revision: CBC/PKCS5 values sourced from BlowfishCipherService Javadoc table instead of DefaultBlockCipherService constructor. DefaultBlockCipherService.java constructor now read — direct source confirmation obtained.

new_evidence_record.explanation: DefaultBlockCipherService constructor directly confirms: modeName=OperationMode.CBC.name() and paddingSchemeName=PaddingScheme.PKCS5.getTransformationName(). BlowfishCipherService extends DefaultBlockCipherService and does NOT override modeName or paddingSchemeName — only overrides setInitializationVectorSize(64). Therefore BlowfishCipherService inherits CBC mode and PKCS5Padding from this constructor. Evidence elevation concern is now resolved: CBC and PKCS5 are confirmed from direct source code, not Javadoc.

revised_har.summary: BlowfishCipherService fully confirmed. ALGORITHM_NAME='Blowfish', BLOCK_SIZE=64 bits. DefaultBlockCipherService constructor directly confirms CBC mode (OperationMode.CBC.name()) and PKCS5 padding (PaddingScheme.PKCS5.getTransformationName()) at lines 180-181. BlowfishCipherService overrides only IV size to 64 bits — mode and padding inherited unchanged. No production caller found. 64-bit block size is the primary weak_algorithm concern.
raw_observations: BlowfishCipherService does NOT override modeName or paddingSchemeName
No production caller found in grep sweep
uncertainties: No production caller confirmed — dead code risk; class is in public API but not instantiated in main sources
follow_up_suggestions: Consider deprecating BlowfishCipherService — 64-bit block size makes it cryptographically weak regardless of key size.
Confirm no production configuration file references BlowfishCipherService.
